approach: APPROACH A — Conservative: implement exactly the spec with minimal scope, following existing patterns
steps: 2. Create src/components/activities/SongCoverArt.tsx — image with 🎵 fallback on error
3. Create src/components/activities/SongPlayerControls.tsx — play/pause button + inline error UI with retry

4. Modify src/components/activities/SongPlayer.tsx — add retryCount state, update Howl useEffect deps, add handleRetry, remove imgError, remove early-return error block, replace cover art inline JSX with <SongCoverArt>, replace loading+play inline JSX with <SongPlayerControls>, add new imports
5. Modify src/components/session/GreetingStep.tsx — import audioManager, add second useEffect that calls audioManager.playWordEn('/audio/en/hello.mp3') on mount

6. Update src/__tests__/SongTime.test.tsx — add tests: retry creates new Howl instance (Howl constructor called twice), play button briefly disabled after retry (audioReady=false), GreetingStep audio call on mount
7. Run npm test to verify all tests pass
expected_tests: retry button triggers new Howl constructor call (retryCount mechanism creates fresh Howl)
after clicking retry, song-play-btn is disabled while new Howl loads (audioReady=false)
SongCoverArt renders 🎵 emoji fallback when image onError fires
SongPlayerControls renders ▶ when isPlaying=false, ⏸ when isPlaying=true
risk_areas: Existing 'retry button clears error state' test must still pass — it checks that 'Song unavailable' text disappears after retry; with retryCount approach this still works because useEffect resets audioError=false
React 19 Strict Mode double-invocation of effects may call audioManager.playWordEn twice in dev; acceptable for audio enhancement
The useEffect Howl dependency chain (handleSongEnd, cancelRaf) must remain stable via useCallback — already the case in existing code
Removing early-return audioError block and handling it inline means existing tests that check for song-player testid on error state will now always find it (which is correct)
persistence_strategy: N/A — these are pure UI/audio components. No new data is introduced. SongTime's existing IndexedDB play-count persistence via putSong is unchanged.
error_handling_approach: SongPlayerControls renders inline error UI with Retry button — no silent failures. audioManager.playWordEn has try/catch. After retry, new Howl loaderror will set audioError=true again if second load attempt also fails — error UI reappears.
